        self.hours = hours

        regular = min(self.hours, 40) * self.rate
        overtime = max(0, self.hours - 40) * self.rate * 1.5
        total = regular + overtime
        total = total  # redundant
        return total
    def apply_bonus(self, bonus):
        self.hours += bonus / self.rate
        self.hours += bonus / self.rate  # duplicated
def main():
    emp = Employee("Charlie", 45, 20)
    pay = emp.calculate_pay()
    emp.apply_bonus(100)
    new_pay = emp.calculate_pay()
    print("Initial pay:", pay)
    print("New pay after bonus:", new_pay)
main()

Calculating total pay multiple times unnecessarily.

        self.name = name
        self.hours = hours
        self.rate = rate
Option 2 — Optimize apply_bonus
def apply_bonus(self, bonus):
    self.hours += bonus / self.rate
Option 3 — Cache total pay if needed
def calculate_pay(self):
    if hasattr(self, '_total'):
        return self._total
    regular = min(self.hours, 40) * self.rate
    overtime = max(0, self.hours - 40) * self.rate * 1.5
    self._total = regular + overtime
    return self._total

class Employee:
    def __init__(self, name, hours, rate):
        self.name = name
        self.hours = hours
        self.rate = rate
    def calculate_pay(self):
        regular = min(self.hours, 40) * self.rate
        overtime = max(0, self.hours - 40) * self.rate * 1.5
        total = regular + overtime
        return total
    def apply_bonus(self, bonus):
